title: Bookkeeper
description:
About Us

The Weekend Project is a UK-based company that sells event tickets and merchandise through Shopify. We've been growing rapidly — from around £12k in sales in H2 2024 to over £90k in 2025 — and we need a skilled bookkeeper to make sure our financials are clean, accurate, and fully reconciled before we close out the year.



What You'll Be Doing

This is a focused clean-up project covering our full 2025 accounts and 2026 YTD, with the goal of producing a reconciled balance sheet and P&L we can rely on. Specifically:



Reconcile the full balance sheet as at 31 December 2025 and 31 March 2026

Ensure all Shopify Payments clearing accounts are fully reconciled to zero (or with documented timing differences only)

Post any missing journal entries for Shopify sales, processor fees, discounts, refunds, and shipping income

Cross-check processor fees against official Shopify Payments monthly invoices

Reconcile the Revolut bank account to the QuickBooks general ledger

Review and clean up any mispostings or duplicate entries

Produce a clean P&L for the full year 2025 and YTD 2026 split by product category (tickets vs. merchandise)

Document your work with a reconciliation workpaper for each account





Systems We Use



QuickBooks Online (UK)

Shopify / Shopify Payments

Revolut (main business bank account)

Stripe (payment processor — same as Shopify Payments)





Our Chart of Accounts Structure (you'll need to understand this)

We use a clearing account approach for Shopify income:



Shopify Clearing ? Shopify Payments Clearing (sub-account)

Monthly Sales JEs post gross sales by category (40001 Shopify Ticket Sales, 40002 Shopify Merch Sales), with separate lines for discounts (41001/41002), refunds (42001/42002), shipping income, processor fees, and the net clearing debit

Processor fees come from official Shopify Payments invoices issued the 5th of the following month





Who We're Looking For



Proven experience with QuickBooks Online — you must be comfortable posting and editing journal entries, running reports, and reconciling accounts

Experience with Shopify, Stripe, or e-commerce bookkeeping — this is important; the settlement/payout timing nuances matter

Strong attention to detail — we've already identified specific discrepancies (fee methodology differences, missing JEs) and need someone who can work through these systematically

Able to work independently with minimal hand-holding — we'll give you access and clear documentation of what's been done so far, but we need someone who can take it from there

Clear written English — you'll be providing reconciliation workpapers and brief written summaries of your findings

Experience with UK-based companies is a plus (though not essential)





What We'll Provide



Read/write access to QuickBooks Online

Shopify Payments payout exports and official monthly invoices

Revolut bank statements

A detailed reconciliation workpaper already prepared covering the Shopify Payments Clearing account, so you know exactly where we stand and what's outstanding

Clear documentation of our JE structure with examples





Deliverables



Fully reconciled balance sheet as at 31 Dec 2025 and 31 Mar 2026

Clean P&L for 2025 (Jan–Dec) and 2026 YTD (Jan–Mar) by category

All missing journal entries posted and documented

Reconciliation workpaper for each balance sheet account

A short summary of any issues found and how they were resolved

title: Stencil Designer + Amazon Listing Image Creator
description:
We need a designer who can do **two related things well**:



1. Convert source artwork (provided by us) into clean, cuttable stencil files in Adobe Illustrator

2. Create 2 Amazon listing images per design using our pre-built templates



For each design, you'll:



 Part 1 — Stencil File Prep (Illustrator)



- Take a source file we provide (PNG, JPG, AI, or hand-sketched image)

- Convert it to a clean cuttable stencil in Illustrator:

  - Image trace + clean up paths

  - Add bridges where needed so islands don't fall out (e.g., the inside of an "O" needs to stay attached)

  - Verify cut paths are continuous and laser-ready

  - Save as .ai master file

- Export a flat preview PNG/JPG



Part 2 — Amazon Listing Images (Photoshop or Illustrator)



For each approved stencil design, create **2 listing images** using our pre-built templates:



1. **Main image** — stencil on white background (Amazon required: pure white, 85% frame fill, no text/borders/logos)

2. **Lifestyle image** — stencil rendered as final paint output (e.g., the design painted on a wall, wood, or canvas)



We provide reusable templates with placeholder slots. Your job is to drop the new artwork into the template, not design from scratch every time. Once you're trained on the templates, each listing should take ~10 minutes of image work after the stencil prep is done.



File Delivery



- Master stencil file: `.ai` (Illustrator)

- Preview: `.png` at 2000x2000px on white

- Listing images: 2x JPGs at 2000x2000px, named per our SKU convention

- All saved to a designated Google Drive folder per SKU



Required Skills & Experience



- **3+ years Adobe Illustrator** — image trace, pathfinder, bridge creation, vector cleanup

- **2+ years Adobe Photoshop** — template-based design work, image compositing

- **Stencil/laser cut experience preferred** — you understand bridges, islands, kerf, and continuous cut paths. (If you've never done stencils, you must have die-cut, vinyl-cut, or similar vector prep experience.)

- **Amazon listing image experience** — you've made Amazon main images before and know the rules (pure white background, 85% frame fill, no text/borders/logos)

- **Reliability and consistency** — same look, same quality, every time



Nice to Have



- Familiarity with arts/crafts/DIY product categories

- Background in surface design, textile design, or pattern work

- Ability to suggest design improvements (e.g., "this skull will lose detail at small sizes — recommend simplifying these areas")



Time Commitment



- ~10-25 hours per week, flexible schedule



---



**Note:** We are NOT looking for someone to design original artwork from scratch. We provide source images and concepts. Your job is to take that input, convert it to production-ready files, and create listing imagery using our templates.

title: Social Media Video Editor / Content Director — AI-Forward Dental Brand
description:
We're 5D Smiles + WakeWell Sleep Solutions — a dental implant + sleep medicine practice in Downey, California. We're hiring a remote Social Media Video Editor / Content Director who knows AI tools and can think strategically — not just cut and assemble.



This is the opposite of "edit what we send you and take 3 days per video." We want a self-directing creator who can come up with ideas, find what's working in our niche, ship 5-10 short-form videos per week, and use every AI tool in their toolbelt to do it faster than the next editor.



WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO:

- Edit short-form vertical (TikTok, Reels, Shorts) for both 5D Smiles (dental implants) and WakeWell Sleep (oral-appliance therapy)

- Come up with the video IDEAS — not just edit what's sent

- Build hook strategies that work for our niche (dental + sleep)

- Use AI for ideation, scripting, captions, b-roll, voice cloning — whatever saves time

- Ship 5-10 finished videos per week, every week

- Track performance and iterate based on what hits



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

- Proof you've grown an audience yourself OR an account you've run (exact follower count + starting count)

- Daily AI use — Claude Code, ChatGPT, Runway, Pika, Sora, ElevenLabs, Submagic, Opus Clip, Descript

- (Bonus) Graphics design ability — Photoshop, Canva, Figma — for thumbnails + tiles

- (Bonus) Account directorship — can you plan a whole month of content for one brand?



WHAT YOU'LL GET:

- Direct mentorship on the AI-creator stack — we use these tools every day at the practice

- Long-term role — we're building a content team, not hiring a one-off freelancer

- Real growth path: editor ? senior editor ? content director (manage other editors)

- Work with a CEO (Dr. Henry Qiu) who is genuinely AI-forward and ships fast — no committee, no slow approvals



HOW TO APPLY:

Apply at 



Our application is short. The most important questions:

- Drop a link to your best work

- Pitch us 5 video ideas if we gave you 30 days running our channels

- Tap any standout signals (own audience 10k+, viral hit 100k+, 50+ videos shipped, healthcare niche)



Plan ~10-15 minutes for the full apply flow. Strong candidates get a reply within 48hr.



— Dr. Henry Qiu, Founder

5D Smiles + WakeWell Sleep Solutions, Downey CA

title: Account Coordinator
description:
Account Coordinator

Headspace Media



Reports to: Account Manager / CEO

Location: Remote

Type: Full-time or part-time (20–40 hours/week)

Compensation: Based on experience 



Role Summary

The Account Coordinator is the organizational backbone of client account management at Headspace Media. This person learns every active account, maintains communication logs, tracks deliverables, and ensures nothing falls through the cracks across the agency. They don’t own the client relationship — the Account Manager does. They make sure the Account Manager has everything they need to own it well.

Think of this role as the person who always knows where everything is. What’s the status of the Smith’s Roofing report? When was the last time we
ed the Bob’s Plumbing client? Did the Petting Zoo creatives get approved? Has anyone followed up on that proposal? The Account Coordinator knows, or knows exactly where to find out.

This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working across multiple tools and platforms. Strong written English is essential. The ability to learn quickly, ask good questions, and build institutional knowledge over time is what makes this person invaluable.



What This Role Rewards

Organization and attention to detail — tracking dozens of moving pieces across multiple accounts without dropping anything

Proactive follow-up — noticing when something is overdue, unresponded to, or missing before anyone asks

Written English fluency — clear, professional, and accurate written communication (internal, not client-facing initially)

Tool proficiency — comfort learning and working across multiple platforms quickly (CRM, project management, analytics dashboards)

Reliability — showing up consistently, doing what you said you’d do, and closing the loop without being reminded

Curiosity — wanting to understand how accounts work, why decisions are made, and what makes clients happy



Core Responsibilities

Account Knowledge & Documentation

Learn every active client account — who they are, what services they receive, who on the team works on their account, what the current status is

Maintain an account reference document for each client — key contacts, services, contract terms, meeting cadence, communication preferences, and current priorities

Build and maintain a master status tracker across all accounts — what’s in progress, what’s pending client approval, what’s overdue, what’s upcoming

Become the person who can answer “what’s happening with [client]?” at any time



Communication Tracking & Follow-Up

Monitor client communication channels 
, ClickUp, GHL, Client Portal) and log key updates, requests, and decisions

Track response times — flag any client message that hasn’t been responded to within 24 hours

Draft follow-up reminders for the Account Manager — “We haven’t contacted [client] in 10 days” or “[Client] asked about X on Tuesday and we haven’t responded”

Maintain a communication cadence tracker — when was the last proactive touchpoint with each client?



Deliverable Coordination

Cross-reference ClickUp tasks with client commitments — is everything that was promised actually in the production queue?

Track deliverable due dates and flag anything at risk of missing a deadline before it’s late

Verify that completed deliverables were actually sent to the client — not just marked done in the PM tool

After deliverables are sent, confirm client receipt and log any feedback



Meeting Support

Prepare the Client Pulse summary before Monday meetings — one-line status per account for the Account Manager to present

Take notes during CEO meetings and distribute action items with owners and deadlines within 24 hours

Track action items from all meetings and follow up on overdue items

Flag any recurring themes or patterns across meetings (e.g., same client appearing in “yellows” multiple weeks in a row)



Onboarding Support

Assist with new client onboarding logistics — checklists, access setup, document collection, welcome communications

Ensure all onboarding steps are completed and documented before a client is marked “live”

Maintain onboarding templates and update them based on lessons learned



Tools & Systems

GoHighLevel (GHL) — navigate client accounts, understand pipelines and workflows, track communications

ClickUp — monitor task status, cross-reference deliverables, track deadlines

Google Workspace —
 monitoring, Google Drive file organization, document management

TrolleyOS (internal platform) — learn the system as it develops, support data accuracy and client records

Fathom.video — access meeting recordings and transcripts for reference and follow-up



What This Role Is Not

Clarity on boundaries protects both the person in this role and the team.



Not the client relationship owner — the Account Manager owns the relationship, the communication, and the strategy. This role supports that ownership.

Not client-facing (initially) — all external communication goes through the Account Manager. Over time, this may expand to routine client updates under AM direction.

Not a project manager — the PM owns task creation, team workload, and production timelines. This role tracks whether things are on schedule, not why they’re not.

Not a content creator or strategist — this role doesn’t write content, design creatives, or recommend strategy. It tracks and coordinates.

Not a technical role — no coding, no CRM configuration, no ad management. Familiarity with tools is for navigation and tracking, not building.



Ideal Candidate

We’re looking for someone who is:



Obsessively organized — you track things for fun, you notice when something is missing, you can’t sleep if a task isn’t logged

A strong written communicator in English — your messages are clear, professional, and complete. Grammar and spelling matter to you.

Proactive, not reactive — you don’t wait to be asked. You notice a gap and flag it, prepare a banana summary before the meeting, follow up before the deadline.

Comfortable with ambiguity — you’re joining a growing company. Not everything is documented yet. You’re the kind of person who builds the documentation as you learn.

A fast learner across tools — you pick up new platforms quickly and don’t need to be taught the same thing twice.

Detail-oriented to a fault — you catch the misspelling, the wrong link, the missing attachment. Every time.

Available during U.S. Eastern Time business hours (at least 4–6 hours of overlap)



Experience in a marketing agency, digital marketing, or client services environment is a strong plus but not required. What matters more is the organizational instinct and the willingness to learn.



A Day in the Life

Here’s what a typical day might look like for this role:



Morning: Review all client communication channels. Log any overnight messages or requests. Flag anything that needs AM attention. Update the master status tracker.

Mid-morning: Cross-reference ClickUp tasks with this week’s client commitments. Flag any deliverables at risk of being late. Follow up with tea
mbers on overdue items.

Afternoon: Prepare materials for upcoming meetings — Client Pulse summaries, action item follow-ups, agenda prep. Draft follow-up reminders for the AM.

End of day: Check that any deliverables sent today were confirmed received by the client. Update communication cadence tracker. Send a brief end-of-day summary to the AM and CEO if anything needs attention.



What Success Looks Like

The Account Manager never has to ask “what’s the status of [client]?” — you’ve already provided it

No client message goes unanswered for more than 24 hours without someone being flagged

Deliverables don’t slip through the cracks — if something was promised, it’s tracked, and if it’s late, you raised it before anyone noticed

Meeting action items are documented and followed up on — nothing dies in the meeting notes

The CEO’s operational load decreases — fewer things to check, fewer gaps to fill, fewer questions to chase

Within 90 days, you know every client well enough to brief someone on any account fro
mory



Growth Path

This role is designed as an entry point with real trajectory. As you prove reliability and build client knowledge, the role can grow into:



Account Coordinator > Account Manager — if you develop the client relationship skills, communication presence, and strategic instinct, this role can become the full AM position

Account Coordinator > Operations Coordinator — if your strength is more in systems, processes, and internal coordination, the role can expand into broader operations management



The key to growth is the same as in every role at Headspace Media: reliability, initiative, and ownership. Do the task. Then do the Next 10%.



How to Apply

Send a brief introduction, just tell us who you are in your own words and why you are a fit for this role. Make sure you include the subject line: Account Coordinator - [fruit hidden somewhere in the job description]



Headspace Media is a small, growing company built on the values of excellence, integrity, and service. We care deeply about our clients and our team. If that resonates with you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Amazon Catalog Manager (Seller Central Expert)
description:
Full-Time | Remote | Long-Term Position



We are a growing U.S.-based premium brand seeking an experienced Amazon Catalog Manager to take full ownership of our Amazon listings, catalog health, and optimization strategy.



This is not an entry-level role. We are looking for someone who understands Amazon deeply — including catalog structure, indexing, variation strategy, flat files, and Seller Central troubleshooting.



If you are analytical, proactive, and focused on improving listing performance, we want to work with you long-term.



---



## Your Role



You will be responsible for the overall health, optimization, and performance of our Amazon catalog. Your work will directly impact organic rankings, conversion rates, and revenue growth.



You will fully own the catalog.



---



## Core Responsibilities



### Catalog Management



* Create and optimize listings (Titles, Bullet Points, Descriptions, Backend Search Terms)

* Build and manage parent-child variations correctly

* Perform bulk updates using flat files (required skill)

* Fix suppressed listings and catalog errors

* Resolve stranded inventory issues

* Maintain listing compliance with Amazon policies

* Manage and update A+ Content



---



### Amazon SEO & Optimization



* Conduct advanced keyword research (Helium 10, DataDive, or similar)

* Improve indexing and organic rankings

* Monitor keyword performance and adjust strategy

* Increase conversion rates through listing improvements

* Analyze competitors and identify ranking opportunities



---



### Image Strategy & Coordination (No Design Required)



You will own the image optimization process, but you will not personally design graphics.



* Conduct image audits to improve click-through rate and conversion rate

* Analyze competitor images and identify improvement opportunities

* Create detailed image briefs for designers (infographics, lifestyle images, comparison charts, feature callouts)

* Coordinate with designers and photographers to ensure brand consistency

* Ensure all images comply with Amazon policies

* Upload and manage image order within listings

---



### Seller Central Management



* Open and manage Seller Support cases

* Handle Brand Registry-related issues

* Monitor listing health dashboards

* Ensure correct category placement and attributes



---



## Required Experience



* 3+ years managing Amazon Seller Central

* Strong flat file experience (mandatory)

* Deep understanding of Amazon SEO and indexing

* Experience fixing suppressed listings

* Strong variation strategy experience

* Strong written English skills

* Ability to work independently without constant supervision



---



## Preferred (Bonus) Experience



* Experience working with branded or premium products

* Experience with Brand Registry

* Experience significantly improving conversion rates

* Strong data-driven mindset



---



## Compensation



* Competitive monthly salary (based on experience)

* Performance-based bonuses

* Long-term opportunity

* Growth potential into senior operations role



---



## Work Details



* Full-time

* Remote

* Flexible schedule (some overlap with U.S. hours preferred)

* Long-term commitment required (no short-term freelancers)



---



## How to Apply (Important)



To be considered, please send:



1. A short summary of your Amazon experience

2. 2–3 examples of listings you have optimized

3. A brief explanation of how you improve indexing

4. Your expected monthly salary

5. The phrase “CATALOG OWNER” at the top of your application

6. SEND US LINK TO YOUR CV/RESUME WITH WORK HISTORY



Applications without the required phrase will not be reviewed.



---



We are building a serious team and looking for someone who takes ownership, thinks strategically, and executes at a high level.



If that’s you, apply now.

We’re looking for someone who thrives behind the scenes—organizing, building, and launching the systems that keep everything running.

title: Motion Graphics and Video Editor for Top Podcast Agency
description:
Are you a talented motion graphics designer and video editor with an eye for creativity and design?



Do you enjoy podcasts, YouTube, and dream of working with top-level shows?



Longwave is a UK-based podcast production agency on the lookout for an exceptional creative to join our growing Philippines team. You'd be our 5th PH teammate, working alongside 4 Filipino editors already on the team.



We're seeking a creative individual who can contribute their unique flair and design aesthetic across both motion graphics AND video editing, straddling the two confidently.



WHO WE ARE



At Longwave, we're privileged to work with some of the world's most renowned podcasts (you will know them). We're are the UK's leading video podcast agencies, producing YouTube videos, podcasts, and shortform content for top shows globally. Our Philippines team is an essential part of how we deliver. We work closely, grow people up, and take pride in the quality of what goes out.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



- Help create YouTube videos, storytelling intros, and shortform content

- Collaborate on podcast and YouTube video editing assignments for some of the top shows globally

- Help design identities for podcasts and other creators

- Actively shape the visual identity of our clients' brands

- Deliver exceptional results within tight deadlines, with a supportive team behind you every step of the way



OUR TIERED GROWTH SYSTEM



We run a clear 5-tier progression so you always know what "getting better" looks like and what it unlocks. Each tier comes with a pay bump, with reviews no favouritism, no guesswork.



Tier 1 — Short Form / Basic Video Editing: Cuts short-form clips and handles basic video edits to brief.

Tier 2 — Basic Motion Graphics: Applies and adapts existing motion templates; handles simple animation work.

Tier 3 — Motion Graphics: Full motion graphics capability, builds custom motion work and new templates.

Tier 4 — Storytelling: Crafts storytelling intros and narrative edits; moves beyond execution into creative direction.

Tier 5 — Elite Storytelling / Unassisted Motion Graphics: Top-level creative with full autonomy. Delivers high-end storytelling pieces and complex motion graphics independently.



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



- 1+ years video editing experience

- Proficiency in After Effects, Premiere Pro, and the entire Adobe Creative Suite

- A genuine interest in video podcasts and a willingness to share and discuss ideas with the team

- Good written English for briefs, Slack, and Frame.io comments

- Reliable internet and a machine that can handle 4K footage

- Available full time, Monday–Friday, with overlap with UK hours



Qualifications aren't our focus, we're more interested in your skills and what you can bring to the table.



WHAT WE OFFER



- An opportunity to learn and grow in a supportive environment

- Involvement in diverse projects that challenge and inspire

- A chance to contribute your voice and vision to the world of podcasting

- A clear 5-tier growth path, not a ceiling



WORK SETUP



- 37.5 hours/week, full time

- Remote (work from home)

- Some overlap with UK hours required — exact schedule discussed in interview





COMPENSATION



Starting salary: £2.50 (200php) per hour to £8 (650php) per hour



HOW TO APPLY



Please attach your portfolio showcasing your best motion graphics and editing work, and let's start a conversation! We love video, so feel free to submit a video CV.

In your message, also tell us:



Why this role fits you and which tier you think you'd start at (be honest — we test fairly and place accurately).

A podcast or YouTube show you love and why.



Put the word WAVELENGTH at the top of your message so we know you read the full post. Applications without a portfolio or reel will be skipped.

We're excited to hear from you and explore how you can help shape the future of podcasting with us.

title: Senior bookkeeper
description:
We are an Australian CFO advisory and accounting practice working with a growing portfolio of small to medium businesses across multiple industries. Our team delivers bookkeeping, tax, compliance, and strategic CFO advisory services to clients who rely on us for accurate, timely financial management. 

This is not a junior data entry role. We are looking for an experienced bookkeeper who can work independently, take full ownership of their client base, and be trusted with complex accounts from day one.

What You Will Be Doing

Client Bookkeeping — Day to Day Execution

• Perform weekly bank reconciliations in Xero across multiple client accounts

• Match and process transactions using Hubdoc — review, code, and attach supporting documents

• Code transactions accurately to correct accounts using client chart of accounts

• Handle exceptions — suspense coding, personal expenses, unidentified transfers — following firm SOP

• Maintain the exceptions log and flag items requiring partner review

• Ensure Xero unreconciled transactions at end of each weekly cycle

Quality and Compliance

• Ensure all work follows the firm’s documented Standard Operating Procedures

• Maintain GST coding accuracy across all client accounts

• Flag balance discrepancies immediately — do not proceed without partner clearance

• Ensure all client data is handled with strict confidentiality



What We Are Looking For

Non-Negotiable Requirements

• Xero certified — you must be able to demonstrate this

• Minimum 2 years bookkeeping experience

• Prior experience working for an Australia -essential

• Solid understanding of Australian GST, BAS, and compliance requirements

• Accounts payable and receivable management

• Prior experience in a remote-first Australian accounting firm

• Familiarity with Australian payroll and STP reporting

• Strong written English — professional client facing communication standard

• Available to work Australian business hours (AEST / AEDT) full time

• Stable high speed internet connection and professional home office setup

What We Offer

• Competitive salary — PHP 50,000 – 60,000 per month based on experience

• Stable full time hours with a growing Australian practice

• Direct working relationship with the managing partner — not a faceless agency

• Long term role with clear growth pathway as the firm scales

• Supportive, professional team culture with clear systems and processes

How to Apply

Applications without Xero certification and Australian practice experience will not be considered. Please do not apply if you cannot commit to full time Australian business hours.

title: Welcome to K-POP Universe: Office Admin Role (Entry-Level) for E-Commerce – Join the Beat!
description:
- Recruitment validity period: Until 3 business days from the date of the first recruitment announcement

- A PH VA team of more than 5 people has already been formed, and business collaboration is possible using Tagalog in a comfortable atmosphere.

- Providing educational tutorial videos and operating a mentorship program to help new comers quickly adapt



[About Us]

* Company Introduction (Video):

youtu.be/dO2mWWZANx8



[Join Summitree and Write Your K-POP Legacy Alongside Filipinos]

Summitree envisions leveraging their Filipino employees' intelligence and English fluency to expand globally while sharing their passion for K-POP. They highlight the Philippines' crucial role in their expansion plans, emphasizing collaboration with their Filipino team to make a mark in the K-POP world. Joining Summitree means joining a multicultural team (Korea, Philippines, Mongolia, Others) with a shared goal of making K-POP history.



[Responsibilities]

We are looking for a “Full-Time” Office Admin to handle our business.

You will be doing a variety of tasks, almost all of which we will create tutorials for you.

1. Order fulfillment and shipping label generation

You will be responsible for gathering and processing customer orders accurately and efficiently. This includes coordinating with third-party logistics (3PL) providers for inbound and outbound shipments to ensure smooth operations.

2. Product listing and scheduling

As a distributor of K-POP records and merchandise, you will be involved in registering new products into our system. This includes managing inventory, updating product information, and ensuring accurate data entry. Additionally, you will be responsible for scheduling release dates and managing timelines using tools like Google Calendar.

3. Customer service support

providing excellent customer service is crucial to us. You will be responsible for addressing customer inquiries, resolving issues, and ensuring customer satisfaction. Your communication skills and problem-solving abilities will play a vital role in maintaining strong relationships with our valued customers.



[Required qualifications & skillset (must have)]

* Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

* Experience in puchasing K-POP Album or knowledge of K-POP related products (album, merchandise)

* Idea of how to promote K-POP albums and gain traffics in our shopping mall in PH online K-POP community for each artists



[Desired qualifications & skillset (nice to have)]

* Experience in handling customer service

* Experience in Photoshop

* Experience in
 Ads or Google Ads

* Knowledge and Experience of Microsoft Excel & Familar with Using Various Formula

* Experience in experience in Amazon Business (Familiar with Interface and function) or other E-commerce Platform



[Compensation & Employment Terms]

Position Classification:

This is an entry-level position. All candidates, regardless of prior experience, will undergo structured training under a designated mentor to adapt to our internal systems and operational standards.



• Payment Term: Monthly basis

• Probationary Period: 6 months

o Months 0–3: USD 280/month

o Months 4–6: USD 300/month, subject to satisfactory performance evaluation after the first 3 months

o The probation period may be shortened if you demonstrate fast work adaptation and receive an excellent evaluation. (This has occurred in multiple past cases.)



• After Probation Evaluation (Regularization): USD 300/month

o Performance & Total Compensation Structure:

Upon regularization, employees receive a guaranteed 13th Month Pay and at least one annual performance bonus equivalent to one month’s base salary during peak sales seasons. Additional bonuses may be granted based on company performance. When annualized, the total compensation is equivalent to approximately USD 350–380 per month.

o Continued employment and salary maintenance are subject to overall performance evaluation.

o If performance does not meet expectations, adjustments may be made in accordance with company standards.

• Additional Compensation:

o Performance-based incentives may be provided based on work attitude, responsibility, consistency, and overall contribution.



• After Regularization:

o Annual contract renewal is standard

- Salary adjustments may be made based on performance, teamwork, and overall evaluation.

o If overall performance—including work capability, teamwork, reliability, and internal evaluations—is considered outstanding, salary increases may be granted within a shorter period. (This has occurred in multiple past cases.)

o Exceptional performance may also lead to early promotions or salary increases prior to the annual review.



[Perks & Benefits]

1. Regular Employee Benefits

- 13th Month Salary provided

- 10 additional paid leave days upon regularization

2. Performance-Based Incentives

- Additional incentives may be provided during high-performance sales periods

(Example: major comeback seasons for artists such as BTS or Stray Kids)

3. Birthday Benefit (After 6 Months of Employment)

- Receive one free K-POP album of your favorite artist on your birthday

4. Paid Holidays (Regardless of Employment Status)

- January 1st (New Year’s Day) and Christmas Day are paid holidays

- Applies to both probationary and regular employees

5. Long Service Incentive Program

- 1 Year Service: PHP17,000

- 3 Year Service: PHP22,500

- 5 Year Service: PHP28,000



[How To Apply for the job]

Just Fill out 10 Simple Questions through Google Form






Interview via google meet will be conducted after first screening and candidates will be notifed to their
s or
 



Thank you

Summitree

We run one of the largest Roblox content websites. Our biggest section is our game codes directory, and we need a reliable person to keep it accurate and up-to-date.



Job details:



- 30 hours/week minimum (6 hours/day × 5 days, flexible hours, work whenever suits you)

- 2-month gig to start, with strong potential to convert to long-term part-time for the right person

- $10/hr USD

- Remote, anywhere with good English



What you'll be doing:



You'll work from a custom dashboard (the "CUN") that automatically detects new 
 codes from
, 
, etc and creates alerts for you to review. Your job is to verify each one. For every alert, you'll:



1. Open the relevant Roblox game

2. Test the code to confirm it works

3. Lightly edit the auto-generated description if needed (clear, well-written English)

4. Approve it (or reject it if invalid).



You'll handle 100+ codes/day, so this is a high-volume role where efficiency matters. The work runs in daily batches. No need to be always-on or respond to alerts in real time.



You'll have full authority to approve or reject codes yourself. For edge cases, you are expected to communicate with the team to work out the best way to handle these. We'll figure out the right call together.



What we need from you:



- Roblox familiarity: you don't need to know every game, but you should be comfortable with the platform, navigating games, and redeeming codes

- Strong written English: you'll be lightly editing code descriptions, so your English needs to be clean and natural

- Gaming-capable PC: Roblox needs to run smoothly for you to test codes efficiently. A laggy machine will make this job miserable

- Stable, fast internet: you'll be loading games all day

- Comfortable with computers: keyboard shortcuts, copy/paste, switching between tabs/games quickly. We shouldn't need to explain the basics

- Good judgment and attention to detail: wrong codes ending up on the site is the worst-case outcome we're trying to avoid

- Reliability: hitting your 30 hours and showing up consistently matters more than working specific hours



Nice to have:



- An established Roblox account with playtime history (preferred but not required)



Tools and reimbursements:



- We'll communicate via Slack with occasional video calls

- If a game ever requires Robux to test a code, we'll reimburse you, never spend your own money on this



Why this is worth applying for:



This starts as a 2-month gig, but we're explicitly looking for someone we can keep on long-term as a part-time role. If the initial contract goes well, this becomes a stable, ongoing position. You'll be working directly with me and a small remote team on one of the biggest Roblox websites in the world.



Please answer the following questions in your cover letter:



1. Describe your computer specs and your internet speed (run a speedtest.net test and paste the result)

2. What hours of the day do you usually work? (Just so we know your timezone/availability)

3. In one short paragraph, why do you think you'd be good at this role?

4. What's your favourite Roblox game?

5. Have you ever claimed codes in Roblox games, and if so, what's a code you remember claiming?

title: Shopify Product Management Specialist
description:
Shopify Product Management Specialist (Full-Time | Work From Home | AU Hours)

About the Role



We’re looking for a Shopify Product Management Specialist to join our growing team.



This is a full-time, long-term role for someone based in the Philippines who is comfortable working Australian business hours (AEST).



You’ll take ownership of our Shopify store—making sure everything is accurate, organised, and ready to sell. Your work will directly support our sales team, helping them focus on closing deals instead of fixing product or stock issues.

mber!

title: Bookkeeper
description:
We're a growing high-end bicycle retailer in the US (The Bicycle Station, based in Indiana) looking for a reliable, detail-oriented bookkeeper to take ownership of our monthly books. This is a long-term, part-time role (~20 hrs/week) working fully async with our CEO and operations team. Solid QuickBooks experience required.



About Us

The Bicycle Station (TBS) is a specialty cycling retail and e-commerce business in Columbus, Indiana. We sell custom bike builds, parts, accessories, and apparel through our retail store, Shopify storefront, and online marketplaces. We're in active growth mode and need a sharp bookkeeper to keep our financial backbone clean and reliable.



What You'll Do

You will own the monthly close and day-to-day bookkeeping for the business. Specifically:

- Record and categorize daily transactions in QuickBooks Online

- Reconcile bank, credit card, merchant processor (Shopify Payments, etc.), and loan accounts monthly

- Manage AP — enter bills, track vendor payments, follow up on outstanding invoices

- Maintain accurate COGS and inventory adjustments in coordination with our operations team (we use Lightspeed POS, with a planned migration to Shopify POS)

- Close the books monthly and deliver clean financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) by the 10th of the following month

- Maintain our custom GL chart of accounts and ensure consistent categorization

- Flag anomalies, miscategorizations, or cash flow concerns proactively

- Coordinate with our outside CPA at year-end for tax prep

- Support ad-hoc financial requests (vendor reconciliations, expense audits, etc.)



How We Work

This is a fully async role. You'll communicate with us via
 and Slack — no daily check-ins, no Zoom calls required outside of an occasional monthly review if needed. We value clear written communication, proactive flagging of issues, and people who can run their own playbook without constant supervision. You set your own hours within the week as long as the work gets done and monthly close is on time.



Required Skills & Experience

3+ years of bookkeeping experience for US-based businesses

Strong working knowledge of QuickBooks Online (no certification required, but you should be confident in it)

Experience with retail and/or e-commerce bookkeeping (inventory, COGS, merchant processor reconciliation)

Excellent written English

Detail-oriented, organized, and able to meet monthly close deadlines reliably

Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision

Experience with Excel/Google Sheets for ad-hoc analysis



Nice to Have



Experience with Shopify, Lightspeed, or other retail POS systems

Familiarity with US sales tax filings (multi-state a plus)

Background working with small businesses ($1M–$5M revenue range)



Hours

~20 hrs/week, long-term role



How to Apply

Please include in your application:



A short note about your bookkeeping experience, especially with US retail or e-commerce clients

Years of QuickBooks Online experience

Your current availability (hours/week, time zone overlap with US Eastern Time, if any)

Your expected monthly rate



Bonus: Tell us about a time you caught a bookkeeping error or process improvement that saved a client money or time.



Skills Required (tags for the form):

Bookkeeping, QuickBooks, Accounting, Financial Reporting, Excel, English Speaking, Attention to Detail

title: Construction Bookkeeper
description:
JOB RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:



Client Accounting Services:

*Serve as the primary point of contact for multiple construction clients, ensuring their accounting needs are met with excellence and reliability.

*Mentor and train accounting tea
mbers within our firm, promoting professional growth and embodying our values-driven culture.

Partner with our client accounting services (CAS) team and clients to identify opportunities for process improvement and *increased efficiency.

*Stay abreast of industry trends, tools, and best practices in construction accounting to provide forward-thinking solutions for our clients.

*Lead efforts to refine and improve internal and client-facing workflows, enhancing service delivery.



Accounts Payable Management:

*Oversee the processing of vendor invoices through platforms such as Timberscan (or alternative software), ensuring timely vendor payments and compliance.

*Assist clients in managing payables, receivables, and subcontractor compliance, including tracking lien waivers and insurance documentation.

*Manage trade partner payment workflows, including compliance verification and coordination with project owners for fund releases.

*Resolve invoice and payment discrepancies, collaborating with tea
mbers and trade partners.



Compliance Oversight:

*Maintain and monitor compliance checklists for trade partners, ensuring adherence to regulatory and contractual obligations.

*Assist in preparing financial compliance reports and coordinating with external auditors as required.



Accounting Operations:

*Ensure accurate data entry and reconciliation of bank and credit card transactions within accounting systems (e.g., Sage).

*Support month-end and year-end closing processes, including preparation of journal entries and financial reconciliations.

*Prepare reports on accounts payable aging, cash flow projections, and other key metrics.



Team Leadership and Collaboration:

*Train and mentor accounting tea
mbers, fostering professional growth and a culture of accountability.

*Act as a key liaison between accounting, operations, and project management teams to address financial questions and improve processes.



Process Improvement:

*Identify and implement enhancements to accounting workflows to increase efficiency and accuracy.

*Stay updated on industry trends, software tools, and best practices in construction accounting.



Reporting and Communication:

*Provide regular updates to executive leadership on financial performance and compliance.

*Ensure timely and accurate preparation of financial statements, reports, and audits.





EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS REQUIRED:

*Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

*Active CPA license preferred.

*Minimum 5 years of progressive experience in accounting, with at least 2 years in the construction industry.

*Expertise in public accounting, specifically serving construction clients and managing multiple client accounts.

*Strong proficiency in accounting systems (e.g., Sage, Timberscan) and project management tools (e.g., ProCore).

*Deep understanding of construction accounting standards, including WIP reporting, retainage, and cost allocation.

*Passion for delivering exceptional client service and fostering lasting client relationships.

*Excellent organizational and time-management skills to balance multiple client priorities.

*Strong leadership and team development capabilities.

*Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.

*High attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.

*Ability to communicate complex financial concepts to non-financial stakeholders.



Tech Requirements:

*Computer - at least i5 or equivalent with 8gb RAM

*Internet Speed - At least 50 MBPS Download speed



Job Type: Full-time

Schedule: To be Announced

Salary Range: Php 60,000 - Php 90,000 monthly, Depending on experience



Application Instructions

Please fill out this form:


/appqNAvkEQ10YFlc3/pagqwxmsDSuQGHrJo/form

After submitting the form, click the apply button and send us a short message on why you would be a good fit for the role.

title: Shopify Ecommerce Lead (Merchandising + Light Development)
description:
About Tipsy Elves



Tipsy Elves is a leading DTC apparel brand known for bold, fun, and irreverent designs across holidays, golf, ski, and vacation categories. We move fast, test constantly, and prioritize revenue-driving execution over perfection.  We’re looking for a Shopify Ecommerce Lead to own the day-to-day performance of our website (
) and ensure it is fully optimized for conversion, merchandising, and seasonal campaigns.



Working Hours & Communication Expectations



- Schedule: Must be available Monday–Friday, aligned with US Pacific Time (PST) business hours

- This will typically be 12:00 AM – 8/9:00 AM Philippines time (Tuesday–Saturday local)

- Communication: Daily collaboration via Slack

- Expected to be highly responsive during working hours

- Participate in regular team check-ins and cross-functional coordination

- This is a highly collaborative role requiring constant alignment with marketing, creative, and leadership teams



Role Overview



This role is responsible for managing and optimizing the 
 experience across product launches, merchandising, promotions, and site performance.



You will be the primary owner of the Shopify storefront, ensuring the site is aligned with marketing campaigns, optimized for revenue, and running smoothly at all times, especially during peak seasonal periods.



This is a cross-functional role that sits at the center of marketing, creative, and development, with approximately: 80% merchandising / frontend execution, 20% light development / technical implementation



Key Responsibilities



Site Merchandising & Product Setup

- Create and launch new products in Shopify, including images, descriptions, and variants

- Build and maintain collection pages for launches, campaigns, and paid traffic

- Own merchandising strategy across key collection pages based on performance, seasonality, and inventory



Homepage & Seasonal Execution

- Own homepage updates, navigation structure, and on-site messaging

- Ensure the site reflects current promotions, best sellers, and key product stories

- Execute fast, high-quality updates during peak periods (Halloween, BFCM, Christmas, etc.)



Promotions & On-Site Marketing

- Launch and manage sitewide promotions, including scheduling, QA, and execution

- Create and manage promo codes and discount structures

- Ensure consistency across homepage, PDPs, cart, and checkout



AOV & Conversion Optimization

- Own and optimize Rebuy implementation across PDP, cart, and post-purchase flows

- Identify and execute opportunities to improve AOV and CVR through upsells, bundles, and messaging



Analytics & Performance Optimization

- Analyze performance across traffic, CVR, AOV, and product-level metrics

- Use Google Analytics and Shopify Analytics to inform decisions

- Continuously test and iterate on merchandising, layouts, and offers



Cross-Functional Collaboration

- Partner with design, marketing, and product teams to align on campaigns and launches

- Help prioritize creative needs based on business impact

- Act as the central hub coordinating site updates across teams



Development & Technical Execution 

- Make light frontend code updates directly in Shopify, including: Editing Liquid templates and Updating sections, snippets, and basic styling (HTML/CSS)

- Implement small site improvements without needing full dev cycles

- Work closely with external dev agency for larger builds, bug fixes, and feature development

- Create and prioritize dev tickets, QA releases, and ensure timely execution

- Own biweekly dev syncs and validate scoped work and invoices



Site QA & Operational Excellence

- QA all site changes before launch

- Monitor for bugs, broken links, and merchandising errors

- Maintain high standards for site accuracy, speed, and UX



Qualifications

- 3+ years of hands-on experience managing a Shopify storefront

- Strong understanding of ecommerce fundamentals (CVR, AOV, merchandising strategy)

- Working knowledge of Shopify Liquid, HTML, and CSS with ability to make basic code changes

- Experience coordinating with developers and managing dev workflows

- Strong analytical mindset and ability to make data-driven decisions quickly

- High attention to detail with fast, accurate execution

- Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate in a remote, async environment

- Experience with tools like Rebuy or similar upsell platforms is a plus

title: Social Media Manager
description:
Here's a clean job post ready for OnlineJobs.ph:



TikTok Clipping VA — Skiing & Snowboarding App (Full-Time)

We are hiring a full-time TikTok Clipping VA for Shredder, a skiing and snowboarding social app based in the US. You will run three TikTok pages, edit short-form clips daily, and help grow our brand to a massive audience.



What You'll Do

You will manage three TikTok clipping pages dedicated to skiing and snowboarding content. Each month, you are responsible for posting 300 videos across those pages, generating at least 1,000,000 combined views, and maintaining a consistent standard of quality across every post. This is a full-time role — expect to produce roughly 10 videos per day.

You will source clips from videos we provide, edit them into engaging short-form content, add our branding, and post daily on TikTok. Instagram and YouTube are handled automatically through a tool we set up together — your only manual posting platform is TikTok.



Requirements



Proven experience editing short-form video content (TikTok, Reels, or Shorts)

Strong eye for what makes a clip engaging — pacing, music, transitions, and hook

Ability to produce high output without sacrificing quality

Reliable internet connection and the ability to work full-time hours

Familiarity with TikTok and how the algorithm rewards consistency

Attention to detail — branding, formatting, and posting standards must be followed exactly





Nice to Have



Interest in skiing, snowboarding, or action sports

Experience with CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar editing tools

Prior experience running multiple social media pages





What We Offer



$1,000 USD per month, paid at the end of each 30-day period

Full-time, fully remote position

Clear expectations — we provide the content, you handle the editing and posting

A fast-growing brand with real traction (100k+ downloads, 60M+ organic views)





Payment Terms

Payment is issued in full each month when all three requirements are met: 300 videos posted, 1,000,000+ combined views, and consistent video quality. We track performance transparently and will communicate with you throughout the month.

title: Video Editor & Food Content Assistant (Cooking + Filming)
description:
???? Taguig (near Parañaque / Bicutan)

?? Must live nearby or be able to commute easily





We’re a small, creative food content team looking for a Video Editor & Food Content Assistant to help create recipe videos for social media.



This is not a typical kitchen role.



It’s a mix of:

* video editing

* cooking

* filming

* content creation





You’ll be working closely with a small team (including one other creator), learning hands-on and helping bring recipes to life on camera.



Why this role is exciting

* Your work will be seen by an audience of 1.5+ million followers

* You’ll create content around trending recipes and food ideas

* You can take home the food you make

* All ingredients are covered

* You’ll be part of a small team where your creativity matters

* Your ideas and feedback will be valued and used in recipe development

* Flexible structure with the ability to increase your earnings based on output





What you’ll be doing

* Plan ahead and purchase ingredients for assigned recipes (costs covered)

* Prepare and cook recipes by following instructions precisely

* Film the cooking process using your phone (capturing key steps and angles)

* Set up shots, angles, and sequences to match the required style

* Plate and finish dishes with a clean, visually appealing presentation

* Review footage and refilm if needed to get the best result (quality matters more than speed)

* Edit short-form videos (Reels / TikTok style)

* Upload final footage and files to Dropbox

* Clean and reset the kitchen between recipes

* Provide feedback on recipes and suggest improvements for future versions





Requirements

* Must own an iPhone 12 Pro or newer (or equivalent with high-quality camera)

* Must have video editing experience (CapCut or similar)

* Must live near Taguig / Parañaque / Bicutan area

* Must be an experienced home cook (confident following recipes independently)

* Must have strong attention to detail and precision, especially with measurements

* Able to follow recipes closely and repeat results consistently

* Comfortable working in a kitchen environment

* Not afraid of hard work (this role can be physical and involves being on your feet)

* Takes ownership of their work and cares about producing high-quality output

* Reliable and consistent

* Comfortable working in a private home kitchen (apartment setting)





Preferred (but not required)

* Experience with baking and desserts

* Experience with pastry-style finishes (e.g. neat frosting, piping, decorating baked goods)

* Good eye for food presentation and plating

* Ability to execute clean finishes (spreading, drizzling, layering)

* Strong knife skills and clean portioning

* Interest in content creation or social media

* Familiarity with vegan or plant-based cooking (bonus)





Work Setup & Pay

* Training: hands-on, paid daily rate (3 days per week)

* You’ll be trained directly within the team

* Filming is currently done in a private home kitchen with a small team

* This role requires a high level of trust, professionalism, and respect for the space





After training:

* Flexible hours (must film during good daylight for best quality footage)

* You are responsible for managing your own output



Expected output:

* Minimum 5 videos per week

* 2–3 videos per day is achievable once trained

* No upper limit on output if quality is maintained





Pay structure:

* $11 USD per basic recipe

* $14 USD per mid-level recipe

* $17 USD per complex recipe

* Small monthly retainer

* All ingredients are covered, and you keep the food you make





Who this is for



Someone who:

* enjoys the creative process

* takes pride in doing things properly

* is patient and willing to redo things to get the best result

* is detail-oriented and precise

* wants to take ownership of their work and output

* is motivated to improve and contribute ideas





To apply



Send:

* 1–2 videos you have edited or a portfolio of your work

* A photo of a finished dish you’ve made



Include the word FOODVIDEO in your application so we know you’ve read everything.

title: Backend Automation & AI Engineer
description:
Role Details

- Full-Time | 8 hours/day | Monday to Friday

- Work Hours: Flexible (Must have at least 4 overlapping hours with 8AM–4PM Pacific Time)

- Location: Remote (must have reliable power, internet, and backup)

- Compensation: PHP 80,000 – 100,000/month depending on experience

- Bonuses: Quarterly performance-based bonuses (after probation)

- Benefits: Wellness Day Off (after probation)



_________



About the Role



We’re looking for a highly skilled backend automation and reporting engineer to join our Growth Ops team.



This role is heavily focused on building and maintaining automations and reporting systems for Sales and Marketing, especially in Google Sheets and Looker Studio. You’ll also be responsible for managing backend integrations (e.g., Zapier, HighLevel workflows, APIs) and ensuring reliable, scalable, and cost-efficient automations.



AI bot ownership (e.g., our ChatGPT-based call review bot) will be part of your responsibilities, but the primary focus of this role is on automation and reporting that directly impact revenue visibility and decision-making.



This is NOT a pure maintenance role. We need someone who can proactively improve systems, catch issues before they cause downtime, and propose solutions that improve efficiency and reduce costs.



_________



Key Responsibilities



Automation & Integrations

• Build, monitor, and optimize automation workflows (Zapier, Make, HighLevel, etc.)

• Troubleshoot and resolve backend errors quickly

• Design scalable automation flows across Sales and Marketing



Sales & Marketing Reporting

• Build, maintain, and improve reporting dashboards in Google Sheets and Looker Studio

• Develop KPI tracking systems for Sales and Marketing performance visibility

• Ensure all reports are accurate, reliable, and actionable



AI Bot Support (Secondary Focus)

• Maintain and enhance our ChatGPT-based AI Call Review Bot

• Improve prompts, workflows, and integrations with CRM/dashboards

• Ensure bot output remains accurate and useful for Sales management



System Reliability & Optimization

• Serve as backup for other Backend Specialists on Growth Ops tech systems

• Audit tech stack for unnecessary costs and propose savings

• Implement process improvements that reduce downtime and increase efficiency



_________



What We’re Looking For



Required

- Advanced skills in Google Sheets (formulas, dashboards, troubleshooting)

- Strong experience building reporting dashboards (Google Sheets & Looker Studio)

- Familiarity with GoHighLevel, ClickFunnels, CRM setup, or similar sales tools

- Proven experience in backend automation tools (Zapier, Make, HighLevel, etc.)

- Ability to handle API integrations (calling, receiving, parsing, and handling responses)

- Strong debugging/problem-solving skills

- Experience with sales operations and CRM data structures



Nice to Have

- Database management (e.g., Firebase)

- Hosting platforms (e.g., 
)

- Experience designing automation at scale

_________



Tools We Use

• Automation: Zapier, Make (Integromat), HighLevel, ClickFunnels

• Reporting: Google Sheets, Looker Studio

• AI (secondary): ChatGPT API, custom workflows

• Backend: Firebase, 

• Collaboration: Slack, Loom, Google Drive



_________



About Sales Possible



At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching businesses to scale their impact through world-class marketing, sales systems, and team training.



We don’t just teach sales—we build sustainable systems that empower both businesses and the people behind them. Our clients range from expert coaches to fast-growing education brands, and we take pride in helping them turn vision into results.



We’re a tight-knit, fast-moving team that values clear communication, ownership, and continuous growth. If you thrive in a proactive, performance-driven environment and want to build cutting-edge reporting and automation systems, you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Sales Operations Project Manager
description:
Base: 80,000 PHP/month + Performance Bonus

Wellness Day Off after probation



We’re looking for a strong, proactive Sales Operations Project Manager who can lead a sales pod, drive accountability across closers and setters, and maintain seamless communication between Sales, Growth Ops, and Tech Ops.



This is a leadership and coordination role — you are the operational backbone that ensures the pod performs at a high level, SOPs are followed, data is accurate, and every tea
mber stays aligned and supported. If you’re the kind of person who naturally takes ownership, thrives in fast-paced environments, and knows how to communicate with clarity and authority, you’ll excel here.



This is a full-time position, requiring consistent availability from 8am to 4pm Eastern Time, with solid internet, power, and backup.



All applicants must complete DISC and IQ assessments before applying.



?



Detailed Job Description Breakdown



Pod Leadership & Daily Operations

• Lead and manage the daily workflow of closers and setters to ensure the sales pod operates smoothly.

• Run daily huddles, weekly check-ins, and pipeline reviews with clear agendas and follow-through.

• Manage pod-level projects (e.g., SOP rollouts, funnel changes, tracking fixes) end-to-end, ensuring on-time execution.

• Monitor pod performance, identify bottlenecks early, and escalate issues with solutions—not problems.

• Own the onboarding and offboarding process for new sales staff, including systems access, SOP orientation, and performance expectations.

• Ensure all pod members maintain clean calendars, proper availability, and updated CRM/task workflows.



Quality Assurance, Accountability & Compliance

• Hold closers and setters accountable for submitting accurate EODs, updating dispositions, and following SOPs consistently.

• Conduct quality checks on sales calls, calendar availability, CRM hygiene, and follow-up sequences.

• Audit all sales data regularly (bookings, show rates, sales, payments, renewals) to ensure accuracy before reporting.

• Flag performance gaps early and coach tea
mbers with clarity, empathy, and firmness.

• Ensure all pod documentation, scorecards, and checklists are consistently maintained and up to date.



Cross-Department Coordination

• Work directly with Front-End Tech Ops, Back-End Automation Specialists, and Growth Ops to resolve tech issues, data discrepancies, or workflow breakdowns.

• Communicate updates clearly and quickly across all stakeholders — closers, setters, Growth Ops, SOMs, Tech Ops, and leadership.

• Serve as the bridge for multi-team projects (automation fixes, tracking repairs, new funnels, offer migrations).

• Maintain a strong working relationship with salespeople (who may be impatient or stressed under pressure) while keeping communication tight, professional, and steady.



Reporting & Performance Management

• Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports for leadership, ensuring all numbers are accurate and validated.

• Track KPIs across the full funnel: leads ? setters ? calls ? sales ? renewals.

• Support leadership in evaluating pod performance, identifying improvement plans, and implementing corrective action.

• Ensure the sales pod meets revenue, activity, and quality targets consistently.



?



Skills & Qualifications

• Project Management experience is mandatory.

(Comfortable managing timelines, action items, dependencies, and follow-through across multiple teams.)

• At least 1–2 years experience in sales operations, sales admin, sales coordination, or a similar leadership/ops role.

• Strong leadership and interpersonal skills — able to hold people accountable with firmness and empathy.

• Excellent verbal and written communication; comfortable leading huddles, giving feedback, and jumping on calls.

• Highly organized, detail-driven, and able to manage multiple moving parts in a fast-paced environment.

• Tech-savvy and comfortable navigating CRM, calendar tools, funnels, spreadsheets, and SOPs.

• Experience working with sales teams (closers/setters) is strongly preferred.

• Solution-oriented, proactive, and able to anticipate problems before they happen.

• Thrives in an environment where communication, speed, and ownership are non-negotiable.



?



If this sounds like you…



If you love driving clarity, supporting high-performance teams, and being the operational heartbeat of a fast-moving sales pod, we’d love to meet you. This role is perfect for someone who wants to grow into higher-level ops leadership while playing a mission-critical role in our sales organization.



?



About Sales Possible



At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching companies to build world-class marketing and sales ecosystems. We’re not just a sales team provider — we architect full-funnel systems, optimize performance, and support our clients with cutting-edge automation, AI, and operations infrastructure.



We work with high-growth education brands and expert coaches, helping them scale sustainably while delivering exceptional customer experiences.



We’re a fast-moving, tight-knit team that values ownership, proactive communication, and continuous improvement. If you want to work in an environment obsessed with growth, clarity, and performance — you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Full-Stack React + Salesforce Developer (Contract ? Full-Time) — Fintech / Private Lending
description:
Australian non-bank private lender. We fund residential and commercial loans through warehouse trusts and run an in-house technology platform built on React, Supabase, and Salesforce (Financial Services Cloud + Finspectra).

Engagement: Starts as a contractor (remote, AU business hours overlap preferred). Strong performers transition to a full-time role within 3–6 months.



What you'll do

You'll join a small, fast-moving build led by our MD. You'll own real features end-to-end across a suite of internal apps — a credit workbench, sales CRM, broker portal, executive dashboards, and a public website. Day one priorities:



Diagnose and fix Salesforce ? Supabase sync issues (Finspectra fsCore__ objects, lending applications, customers, collateral, decisions, checklists)

Extend existing React apps — new features, polish, performance, bug fixes

Build new modules within the monorepo following our established patterns

Wire up integrations (Microsoft Graph / SharePoint, Salesforce REST/Bulk APIs, Azure Document Intelligence)



Must-have skills



3+ years React (hooks, state management, component architecture). We use React 18 + Vite, inline styles with shared design tokens, no Tailwind, no router — state-based nav

Strong PostgreSQL and Supabase experience — RLS policies, migrations, PostgREST, JSONB columns, auth (Azure AD OAuth + MFA aal2)

Salesforce integration experience — REST API, SOQL, Flow, Financial Services Cloud or any managed package (Finspectra a big plus)

Comfortable reading and extending a codebase of 2,000–3,000 line files without breaking things

Excellent written English — you'll work async with the MD via PRs and chat



Nice to have



Netlify Functions, Puppeteer / headless Chromium for PDF generation

Microsoft Graph API, SharePoint document storage

Australian financial services or private credit domain knowledge

Experience with Salesforce Flow, round-robin assignment, lead triage



What we offer



Competitive contractor rate (PHP, paid weekly or fortnightly via Wise / direct deposit)

Clear path to full-time employment.

Real ownership — you'll see your code in production within days, not months

Direct line to the MD, no middle management



To apply

Send: (1) GitHub or portfolio link, (2) one paragraph on a Salesforce or Supabase integration you debugged or built, (3) your contractor day rate in PHP or USD. Shortlisted candidates will complete a short TestGorilla assessment.

title: Short Form Video Editor who #Believes in Entrepreneurs| Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We're trying to solve the world's biggest problem: People don't #Believe in themselves enough. For over 22 years, Evan Carmichael has been dedicated to this mission. The way we're approaching this problem is producing inspirational content at scale. The right video at the right time can completely change someone's life and we want to touch as many lives as possible. People may not have a supportive family or community around them, but our content can help give them the belief they need to chase their dreams and make the world a better place. We want to work with people who also want to make an impact and touch people's lives. In your application, please put ‘I am your next awesome Short Form Video Editor’ in the subject line. The job responsibilities include editing engaging short-form videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts, adding captions and motion graphics, selecting impactful clips, enhancing audio and visuals, staying on top of trends, and organizing video assets. You’ll also collaborate with the team to test different styles and improve performance over time. Creativity, speed, attention to detail, and a strong sense of storytelling are key to success in this role. Please also share the link to your resume and portfolio, what your hourly salary expectations are, and in the first line of your message tell us how many YouTube subscribers Evan Carmichael has, followed by who your favorite entrepreneur of all time is and why. Please make sure to send your job application here on OnlineJobs and not via
. The job is full time (40 hours per week) with a flexible schedule. We would start off with a small project for now and we'd pay you for your ideas and research. We also like to work with people over a long period of time, not just short contracts, and give them opportunities to succeed and do more based on what they want to learn. Hopefully that will be you! Thank you for reading and good luck! Sending lots of love! Irish #Believe

title: Video Editor + Clip Researcher for YouTube Shorts (CapCut)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



I run a YouTube Shorts channel focused on viral sports content — controversies, dramatic finishes, fails, weird rules, "you won't believe what happened" type stories (cycling, marathons, football, triathlon, track, etc.). Looking for a long-term video editor + clip researcher to handle the bulk of the workflow so I can focus on scripting, voiceovers, and posting.



You will be responsible for:

1. Finding raw sports footage from TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube

2. Editing the clips in CapCut Pro following my exact style and process



WHAT YOU WILL GET FRO
 (DAY 1)



Before you start, I will send you 3 files that cover the entire workflow end-to-end. You will not have to figure anything out on your own:



1. Standard Operating Procedure (PDF, 11 pages) — covers everything: channel niche strategy, where to find clips, what keywords/hashtags to use, the script structure, tools (4K Downloader, cobalt.tools, VMake, ElevenLabs, CapCut Pro), step-by-step editing process, music guidelines, upload process, posting strategy, algorithm tips, and full do's & don'ts list



2. Script Project (Word doc) — examples of 15+ scripts I've already used so you can see the exact tone, pacing, and storytelling style (Hook ? Build ? Payoff structure)



3. Instruction Video (MP4) — full screen-recording walkthrough showing exactly how I edit a video in CapCut from start to finish, including voice settings, music levels, overlays, transitions, and timing



If you follow these three files, you will be able to do the job correctly from day 1.



DAILY TASKS



- Find 1 fresh viral sports clips per day from TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube using the keyword/hashtag list in the SOP

- Once I approve a clip, edit it in CapCut Pro following the SOP and instruction video:

  • Add the ElevenLabs voiceover I send you (Mark / Convo AI voice)

  • Sync visuals to the script (when the script mentions something, that thing must be visible on screen)

- Make any revisions I request

- Same-day turnaround required



REQUIRED SKILLS

- Strong English (you will need to understand scripts and communicate with me)

- Solid CapCut Pro experience — comfortable with timeline editing, transitions, audio mixing, overlays, motion tracking, slow-motion, freeze-frames

- Reliable and fast-responding — this is a daily-deadline job

- Active TikTok and Instagram accounts (you will be scrolling feeds for clips)

- Eye for what makes a sports clip viral (controversy, drama, weird endings)



To filter out spam applications, please start your reply with the word "RACEDAY" so I know you actually read this post.



TRIAL PERIOD

SEND THREE raw sports clip links from TikTok, Instagram, or YouTube that you think would work as viral videos for this channel 

Also send me Scripts which you would use for that vid

?
